info.title: Fanart.tv API
info.description:
# Fanart.tv API Documentation

The Fanart.tv API provides access to a vast collection of high-quality images for movies, TV shows, and music artists.

**Website**: [https://fanart.tv](https://fanart.tv)  
**Support**: Join our [Discord community](https://discord.gg/r9VufRk) for help and discussions

## Quick Start

1. [Get your API key](https://fanart.tv/get-an-api-key/)
2. Make requests to `https://webservice.fanart.tv/{version}/{resource}/{id}?api_key=YOUR_KEY`
3. Choose the API version that fits your needs (we recommend v3.2)

## API Versions

The API supports three versions with different response formats:

- **v3** (Legacy): Basic image fields without timestamps or dimensions
- **v3.1**: Adds timestamp (`added`) field to all images
- **v3.2** (Current): Adds dimensions (`width`, `height`) and total image count

### Key Differences Between Versions

| Feature | v3 | v3.1 | v3.2 |
|---------|:--:|:----:|:----:|
| Basic fields (id, url, lang, likes) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Type-specific fields (disc, season, etc.) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Timestamp (`added`) | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Dimensions (`width`, `height`) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Total count (`image_count`) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

### Music API Structure Difference

**IMPORTANT**: The music API has a fundamental structural difference between versions:

**v3 & v3.1** - Albums as Object/Map:
```json
{
  "albums": {
    "album-id-1": { "cdart": [...] },
    "album-id-2": { "cdart": [...] }
  }
}
```

**v3.2** - Albums as Array:
```json
{
  "albums": [
    { "release_group_id": "album-id-1", "cdart": [...] },
    { "release_group_id": "album-id-2", "cdart": [...] }
  ]
}
```

## Authentication

All endpoints require an API key. There are two types of keys that can be sent either as **query parameters** or **HTTP headers**:

### Project API Keys (Project Tier)
- **Required in most cases** - Applications should always send a project key
- For applications and projects (e.g., Plex, Emby, Kodi) that integrate the API
- **Query parameter**: `?api_key=YOUR_KEY`
- **HTTP header**: `api-key: YOUR_KEY`
- Get your project key at: https://fanart.tv/get-an-api-key/ (Project API Keys section)
- 7-day delay for access to new images

### Personal API Keys (Personal Tier)
- **Optional** - Can be sent alongside a project key, or used alone
- For end users who want better access to the API (shorter delay for new images)
- **Query parameter**: `?client_key=YOUR_KEY` (takes priority over `api_key` when both are sent)
- **HTTP header**: `client-key: YOUR_KEY` (takes priority over `api-key` when both are sent)
- Get your personal key at: https://fanart.tv/get-an-api-key/ (Personal API Keys section)
- 2-day delay for access to new images (can be upgraded to VIP for immediate access)

**Important for Project Developers**: When a personal API key is provided, it **bypasses any rate limits** applied to the project key and provides better access to newly added images. We strongly recommend that projects allow users to optionally enter their personal API key. This gives users control over their API access and improves their experience with faster access to new content. Direct users to [get their personal key](https://fanart.tv/get-an-api-key/) in your application's settings.

### Authentication Methods

**Typical Usage: Project Key Only**
```
# Query parameter
https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550?api_key=YOUR_PROJECT_KEY

# HTTP header
curl -H "api-key: YOUR_PROJECT_KEY" https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550
```

**Enhanced Usage: Project Key + Personal Key**
```
# Query parameters (personal key takes priority for tier access)
https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550?api_key=YOUR_PROJECT_KEY&client_key=YOUR_PERSONAL_KEY

# HTTP headers (personal key takes priority for tier access)
curl -H "api-key: YOUR_PROJECT_KEY" -H "client-key: YOUR_PERSONAL_KEY" https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550
```

**Alternative: Personal Key Only**
```
# Query parameter
https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550?client_key=YOUR_PERSONAL_KEY

# HTTP header
curl -H "client-key: YOUR_PERSONAL_KEY" https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550
```

**Note**: When both keys are provided, the personal key determines the access tier (2-day delay instead of 7-day). When both query parameter and header are present for the same key type, the query parameter takes priority.

## Access Tiers

Different subscription tiers have different access to newly added images:

- **VIP**: Immediate access to all images (requires [VIP membership](https://fanart.tv/vip/))
- **Personal**: 2-day delay for new images (free with client_key - Personal API Key)
- **Project**: 7-day delay for new images (free with api_key - Project API Key)

All tiers have unlimited image access (no quantity restrictions).

**Upgrade to VIP** to get immediate access to newly added images! Visit [fanart.tv/vip](https://fanart.tv/vip/) for more information.

## Rate Limits

The API is designed for high performance and **rate limiting is very rarely applied**. Most API keys have unlimited access with no rate restrictions.

However, in rare cases where rate limits are configured for specific keys, the API will return HTTP 429 responses. Clients should be prepared to handle these responses gracefully:

- **HTTP Status**: `429 Too Many Requests`
- **Retry-After Header**: Indicates how many seconds to wait before retrying
- **X-RateLimit-* Headers**: Provide limit details and remaining quota

**Best Practice**: Implement exponential backoff when receiving 429 responses, respecting the `Retry-After` header value.

The rate limiting uses a token bucket algorithm with exponential backoff penalties for repeated violations. If you encounter unexpected rate limiting, please contact support via [Discord](https://discord.gg/r9VufRk).

## Base URL

```
https://webservice.fanart.tv
```

get.description:
Retrieve all available fanart images for a specific movie.

The movie can be identified by either:
- **TMDB ID** (The Movie Database ID) - Recommended
- **IMDB ID** (starting with 'tt') - Will be mapped to TMDB ID

Returns different response formats based on the API version requested.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL (Query Parameter)
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: cURL (Header)
x-codeSamples.source:
curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/550" \
  -H "api-key: YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch - Query)
JavaScript (Fetch - Header)
Node.js (Axios)
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php
$apiKey = 'YOUR_API_KEY';
$movieId = '550';

$url = "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/{$movieId}?api_key={$apiKey}";
$response = file_get_contents($url);
$data = json_decode($response, true);

print_r($data);
?>

x-codeSamples.label: Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
movie_id = '550'

url = f'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/{movie_id}'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

x-codeSamples.label: Go
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'net/http'
require 'json'

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
movie_id = '550'

uri = URI("https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/#{movie_id}?api_key=#{api_key}")
response = Net::HTTP.get(uri)
data = JSON.parse(response)

puts data

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Movie identifier (TMDB ID or IMDB ID with 'tt' prefix)

v32.summary: v3.2 Response (Current - with dimensions and timestamp)

get.description:
Retrieve all available fanart images for a specific TV show.

The TV show is identified by its **TheTVDB ID**.

Returns different response formats based on the API version requested.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/tv/81189?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch)
Node.js (Axios)
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php
$apiKey = 'YOUR_API_KEY';
$tvShowId = '81189';

$url = "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/tv/{$tvShowId}?api_key={$apiKey}";
$response = file_get_contents($url);
$data = json_decode($response, true);

print_r($data);
?>

x-codeSamples.label: Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
tv_show_id = '81189'

url = f'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/tv/{tv_show_id}'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

x-codeSamples.label: Go
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'net/http'
require 'json'

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
tv_show_id = '81189'

uri = URI("https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/tv/#{tv_show_id}?api_key=#{api_key}")
response = Net::HTTP.get(uri)
data = JSON.parse(response)

puts data

200.description: Successful response with TV show images

get.description:
Retrieve all available fanart images for a specific music artist, including album artwork.

The artist is identified by their **MusicBrainz ID (MBID)**.

**Important**: The album structure differs between API versions:
- v3 & v3.1: Albums returned as an object/map keyed by release group ID
- v3.2: Albums returned as an array with explicit `release_group_id` field

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/f4a31f0a-51dd-4fa7-986d-3095c40c5ed9?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch)
Node.js (Axios)
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php
$apiKey = 'YOUR_API_KEY';
$artistId = 'f4a31f0a-51dd-4fa7-986d-3095c40c5ed9';

$url = "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/{$artistId}?api_key={$apiKey}";
$response = file_get_contents($url);
$data = json_decode($response, true);

print_r($data);
?>

x-codeSamples.label: Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
artist_id = 'f4a31f0a-51dd-4fa7-986d-3095c40c5ed9'

url = f'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/{artist_id}'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

x-codeSamples.label: Go
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'net/http'
require 'json'

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
artist_id = 'f4a31f0a-51dd-4fa7-986d-3095c40c5ed9'

uri = URI("https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/#{artist_id}?api_key=#{api_key}")
response = Net::HTTP.get(uri)
data = JSON.parse(response)

puts data

get.description:
Retrieve fanart images for a specific album.

**Important**: This endpoint returns the parent artist data with only the requested album included.
The response structure matches the artist endpoint but filtered to a single album.

The album is identified by its **MusicBrainz Release Group ID**.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/albums/1b022e01-4da6-387b-8658-8678046e4cef?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch)
Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
album_id = '1b022e01-4da6-387b-8658-8678046e4cef'

url = f'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/albums/{album_id}'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

200.description: Successful response with album images (returns artist structure with single album)

get.description:
Retrieve fanart images for a specific music label.

The label is identified by its **MusicBrainz Label ID**.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/labels/e832b688-546b-45e3-83e5-9f8db5dcde1d?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch)
Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
label_id = 'e832b688-546b-45e3-83e5-9f8db5dcde1d'

url = f'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/labels/{label_id}'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

get.description:
Retrieve a list of movies with recently added images.

By default, returns movies updated in the last 2 days. Use the `date` parameter to specify a custom timeframe.

The response is a 90-day pre-generated file filtered based on your parameters.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/latest?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch)
Node.js (Axios)
PHP
x-codeSamples.source:
<?php
$apiKey = 'YOUR_API_KEY';

$url = "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/latest?api_key={$apiKey}";
$response = file_get_contents($url);
$data = json_decode($response, true);

print_r($data);
?>

x-codeSamples.label: Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'

url = 'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/latest'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

x-codeSamples.label: Go
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'net/http'
require 'json'

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'

uri = URI("https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/movies/latest?api_key=#{api_key}")
response = Net::HTTP.get(uri)
data = JSON.parse(response)

puts data

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Unix timestamp to filter results (returns items updated after this date)

get.summary: Get recently added TV show images
get.description:
Retrieve a list of TV shows with recently added images.

By default, returns TV shows updated in the last 2 days. Use the `date` parameter to specify a custom timeframe.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/tv/latest?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch)
Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'

url = 'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/tv/latest'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Unix timestamp to filter results (returns items updated after this date)

200.description: Successful response with latest TV shows

get.summary: Get recently added music artist images
get.description:
Retrieve a list of music artists with recently added images.

By default, returns artists updated in the last 2 days. Use the `date` parameter to specify a custom timeframe.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source: curl "https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/latest?api_key=YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (Fetch)
Python (Requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

api_key = 'YOUR_API_KEY'

url = 'https://webservice.fanart.tv/v3.2/music/latest'
params = {'api_key': api_key}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()

print(data)

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Unix timestamp to filter results (returns items updated after this date)

200.description: Successful response with latest music artists

ApiKeyAuth.name: api_key
ApiKeyAuth.description:
**Project API Key** (Project Tier - 7 day delay)

For applications and projects that integrate the API.

Can be sent as either:
- Query parameter: `?api_key=YOUR_KEY`
- HTTP header: `api-key: YOUR_KEY`

Get your key at: https://fanart.tv/get-an-api-key/ (Project API Keys section)

ClientKeyAuth.name: client_key
ClientKeyAuth.description:
**Personal API Key** (Personal Tier - 2 day delay)

For end users accessing the API directly.

Can be sent as either:
- Query parameter: `?client_key=YOUR_KEY` (takes priority over api_key)
- HTTP header: `client-key: YOUR_KEY` (takes priority over api-key)

Get your key at: https://fanart.tv/get-an-api-key/ (Personal API Keys section)

Upgrade to VIP for immediate access to new images!

UnauthorizedError.description: API key is missing or invalid

RateLimitError.description:
Rate limit exceeded (very rare - most keys have unlimited access)

This response is only returned when a specific rate limit has been configured for an API key. 
Most users will never encounter this. If you receive this response, wait for the duration 
specified in the `Retry-After` header before making another request.

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before retrying

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Your configured rate limit (requests per second)

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining (will be 0 when rate limited)

NotFoundError.description:
Resource not found

**Version-specific behavior:**
- **v3**: Returns HTTP 200 with empty object `{}` (legacy behavior for backwards compatibility)
- **v3.1 & v3.2**: Returns HTTP 404 with error message

oneOf.description: v3.2 response - HTTP 404 with error message
v3.1 response - HTTP 404 with error message
v3 response - HTTP 200 with empty object

likes.description: Number of likes the image has received

disc.description: Disc number (for moviedisc and cdart only)

MovieResponseV32.description: Movie response with dimensions and count (v3.2)

image_count.description: Total number of images for this movie

TVResponseV32.description: TV show response with dimensions and count (v3.2)

image_count.description: Total number of images for this TV show

MusicAlbumV3.description: Album images (v3) - used in object/map structure

MusicAlbumV31.description: Album images (v3.1) - used in object/map structure

MusicAlbumV32.description: Album images (v3.2) - used in array structure with explicit ID

MusicResponseV3.description: Music artist response with albums as object (v3)

albums.description: Albums as object/map keyed by release group ID

MusicResponseV31.description: Music artist response with albums as object (v3.1)

albums.description: Albums as object/map keyed by release group ID

MusicResponseV32.description: Music artist response with albums as array (v3.2)

image_count.description: Total number of images for this artist

albums.description: Albums as array with explicit release_group_id

total_images.description: Total number of images for this movie

LatestTVItem.description: TV show with recently added images

total_images.description: Total number of images for this TV show

LatestMusicItem.description: Music artist with recently added images

total_images.description: Total number of images for this artist
